rationale: A0 shows reg.exe querying the Internet Explorer version key under HKLM, a classic reconnaissance technique used by malware and post-exploitation frameworks to fingerprint the host environment. However, this is a single, isolated event with no corroborating evidence (no network connections, no follow-on process creation, no lateral movement indicators), and the query itself is entirely read-only and non-destructive. The parent process is a plain cmd.exe with no visible arguments, which is mildly suspicious but not conclusive; legitimate software installers and IT scripts also perform this query. The elevated integrity level (High) warrants attention but is not definitive without additional context. Confidence is capped low due to the absence of any correlated evidence items.
